samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description> 
        <parameter name="INIT">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="EDGE">Edge state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="LEVEL">Level state.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">State of the edge detect unit.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state of the edge detect unit.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state of the edge detect unit.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the positive edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active low, the state is initialized to INIT. 
            If the reset signal is not active and the input signal a is 0, the state is set to EDGE. 
            If the input signal a is 1, the state is set to LEVEL. 
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            The next state is determined based on the current state and the input signal a. 
            If the state is INIT and a is 0, the next state is EDGE. 
            If the state is INIT and a is 1, the next state is LEVEL. 
            If the state is EDGE and a is 0, the next state is LEVEL. 
            If the state is EDGE and a is 1, the next state is EDGE. 
            If the state is LEVEL and a is 0, the next state is LEVEL. 
            If the state is LEVEL and a is 1, the next state is EDGE. 
        </combinational>
        <sequential>
            The next state is registered to the state on the positive edge of the clock. 
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            The output signals rise and down are determined based on the current state. 
            If the state is EDGE, rise is set to 1 and down is set to 0. 
            If the state is LEVEL, rise is set to 0 and down is set to 0. 
            If the state is INIT, rise is set to 0 and down is set to 0. 
        </combinational>
    </implementation>
</module>
